description: A Source NEVER acquires a dependency edge, even when it is fed from a reactive. 'Feed this merge cell from that reactive' is an Effect that reads the upstream and calls merge -- the edge belongs to the effect, and the merge cell stays edge-free. This pins the Source/Dependent partition STRUCTURALLY rather than by prose: a node's value comes either from outside it or from upstream of it, write-fed or compute-fed, never both. A binding that implemented feeding as a real edge into the cell would satisfy every value assertion in the corpus while having silently added a fourth node kind -- one that is both writable and dependent, which the partition says cannot exist. Only a degree assertion catches that, which is why this fixture asserts on dependencies_of/dependents_of and not on any value. The same fact explains why no new op is needed for the feeding itself: it is an ordinary effect. Contract: #lzmergefeed.

expect.note: baseline: a fresh merge cell is isolated
THE assertion. The effect gained the edge (it reads a); the merge cell gained NOTHING. A binding modelling the feed as an edge into acc shows dependencies_of acc = 1.
and it stays edge-free after the feed has actually delivered -- an edge created lazily on first delivery would pass the previous step and fail here
1 at creation + 5 on the write, under Sum: the feeding works, and it works WITHOUT the cell having a dependency
